content.text: What Is the AWS Partner Network?

The AWS Partner Network (APN) is a global ecosystem of companies that build products, deliver services, and develop expertise around AWS. By mid-2024, the APN includes over 130,000 partners in 150+ countries, from solo consultants and startups to global systems integrators with tens of thousands of AWS-certified employees.

The APN exists because AWS sells infrastructure, not implementation. When a bank wants to migrate its core banking system to AWS, it needs more than an AWS account — it needs architects who know the bank's regulatory environment, engineers who can refactor legacy COBOL, and project managers who can navigate procurement. AWS's own professional services organization (AWS ProServe) handles some of this work, but the demand far exceeds what AWS can supply directly. APN partners fill that gap.

From a practitioner's perspective, understanding the APN matters for two reasons. First, when your organization evaluates AWS consulting firms or ISV products, the APN tier and specializations of a partner are meaningful signals about capability and commitment. Second, if you work for a company that builds software or delivers AWS services, the APN provides financial benefits, technical resources, and market access that can significantly accelerate your business.
Partner Types

attrs.alt: APN partner types: Technology Partners and Consulting Partners, each with four tiers from Registered to Premier

content.text: The APN divides partners into two primary tracks based on what they do. The diagram above shows both tracks and how tier requirements escalate through Registered, Select, Advanced, and Premier. The tracks are not mutually exclusive — a company can be a Consulting Partner and a Technology Partner simultaneously if it both delivers AWS services and builds products on AWS.
Technology Partners

Technology Partners are independent software vendors (ISVs) and SaaS companies that build products that run on or integrate with AWS. When you purchase a product from Datadog, Splunk, HashiCorp, or MongoDB and that product integrates with AWS services or runs on AWS infrastructure, those companies are typically APN Technology Partners.

The Technology Partner track evaluates companies on the technical maturity of their AWS integration: How deeply does the product use AWS services? Is it built to take advantage of AWS security features (IAM, KMS, VPC)? Has it been validated through the AWS Foundational Technical Review (FTR)? Does it have an active listing in AWS Marketplace? Higher tiers require earned specializations (Competencies or Service Delivery designations) in specific domains like Machine Learning or Security.
Consulting Partners

Consulting Partners are system integrators, managed service providers (MSPs), consultancies, and value-added resellers that design, build, and manage AWS environments for customers. They include global firms like Accenture, Deloitte, Wipro, and Infosys, regional boutiques focused on specific industries or AWS services, and niche consultancies specializing in particular workloads like HPC, IoT, or financial services compliance.

The Consulting Partner track evaluates companies on how much certified expertise they have (number of AWS-certified employees and which certifications), how much annual revenue they generate through AWS-related work (reported to AWS), and the quality of customer outcomes (references and case studies). Higher tiers unlock more co-selling support from AWS, access to marketing development funds, and inclusion in the AWS Partner Finder directory with higher placement.
Partner Tiers and Requirements
Tier
Track
Key Requirements
Typical Company Size
Registered
Both
Sign partner agreement; no minimum certifications or revenue
Any size; day-one entry

Technology
1+ AWS Foundational certifications; 1+ validated solution; sign up for Marketplace
Early-stage ISV

Consulting
2+ AWS-certified employees; $250K+ annual AWS services revenue; 2 customer references
Boutique consultancy

Technology
Multiple validated solutions; active specialization; ARR threshold
Growth-stage ISV
Advanced
Consulting
6+ certified staff; $1M+ annual revenue; more references; 1 specialization
Mid-size SI

Technology

Established ISV with global reach

Consulting

The exact revenue thresholds and certification counts are updated by AWS periodically. The numbers in the table above are representative of the tier structure; check the current APN guide (aws.amazon.com/partners/become-a-partner) for the precise current requirements. Requirements also vary by the partner's home country, since AWS adjusts thresholds for market size.
AWS Competencies and Specializations

AWS Competencies are validations that a partner has demonstrated deep technical expertise in a specific domain, as verified by AWS through a rigorous technical evaluation and customer reference check. Competencies are awarded by domain:
Competency Domain
What It Validates
Security
Expertise in designing and managing secure AWS environments; compliance automation
DevOps
CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, automated operations on AWS
Data and Analytics
Data lake, warehouse, BI, and streaming analytics architectures
Machine Learning
ML model development, deployment, and MLOps on AWS
Migration
Large-scale application and data migrations to AWS
SAP
SAP on AWS architecture and operations
Oracle
Oracle database and application workloads on AWS
Networking
Advanced VPC design, hybrid connectivity, and CDN/edge architectures
Government
Public sector and government compliance environments
Healthcare
HIPAA-compliant architectures and healthcare data integration
Energy
Oil/gas, utilities, and renewable energy workloads

When you are selecting a consulting partner for a project, look for Competencies that match your domain. A partner with the Security Competency has had their security practices independently validated by AWS. A partner with the Migration Competency has demonstrated successful large-scale migration outcomes. Competencies are not self-declared — they require evidence submission and an AWS technical review.
Service Delivery Program

The Service Delivery Program is similar to Competencies but focused on delivering specific AWS services (rather than domains). A partner with the Amazon EKS Service Delivery designation has documented methodology, trained staff, and customer references specifically for Kubernetes deployments on EKS. Service Delivery designations are useful when you need help with a specific service rather than a broader domain.
AWS MSP Program

The AWS Managed Service Provider program is a separate validation for partners that provide ongoing management and monitoring of AWS environments (as opposed to project-based implementations). MSP Partners commit to service level agreements for managed environments, provide 24x7 monitoring, and are evaluated on customer outcomes annually. If you need someone to run your AWS environment operationally rather than build it, look for APN MSP partners.
Benefits for Partners
Technical Benefits
AWS Partner Credits: free or discounted AWS service credits for building and testing solutions (amount increases with tier)
AWS technical support: direct access to AWS technical account managers and solutions architects based on tier
AWS partner sandbox accounts: dedicated AWS accounts for partner training and solution development
Early access to new AWS services: invitation to betas and previews before general availability
AWS training discounts: reduced pricing on AWS official training courses and certification exams
Go-to-Market Benefits
Higher-tier partners gain access to co-selling resources that directly impact revenue:

AWS co-sell (Marketplace channel): AWS sales teams can actively promote partner solutions to AWS customers, including sharing deal registration and opportunity management via the APN Customer Engagements (ACE) program
Marketing Development Funds (MDF): funding for joint marketing activities — events, webinars, paid advertising, case studies — that feature both the partner and AWS
AWS Partner Finder listing: inclusion in the public partner directory where customers search for AWS expertise; Premier partners appear first in search results
AWS case study program: co-branded success stories published on aws.amazon.com that serve as reference material for future customers
re:Invent and event access: sponsor opportunities, speaking slots, and booth space at AWS events based on tier
Financial Benefits
Partner private pricing: some AWS services are available at negotiated discounted rates for partner internal use
AWS partner programs funding: specific programs fund proof-of-concept projects, customer migration assessments, and pilot implementations
Channel reselling: authorized partners can resell AWS services, earning a margin on customer AWS spending
How to Find APN Partners
AWS Partner Finder

The AWS Partner Finder (partners.amazonaws.com) is the official directory of APN partners. You can search by partner type (Consulting/Technology), tier (Premier/Advanced/Select), location, industry, and Competency. The directory shows the partner's AWS certifications count, specializations, and customer ratings. This is the primary tool enterprises use to identify potential consulting partners for new projects.
What to Look for in a Consulting Partner
When evaluating APN Consulting Partners for a project, these signals matter most:
Relevant Competency or Service Delivery designation for your domain or specific service
Number of AWS-certified employees — particularly at the Professional and Specialty level, not just Foundational
Verifiable customer references in your industry and at your scale
Tier (Premier/Advanced): higher tiers indicate more AWS investment and validated capability
AWS ProServe co-delivery relationships: some partners co-deliver with AWS Professional Services on complex projects, which can indicate trust from AWS itself
Geographic delivery capability: ensure they have staff in the regions and timezones where you need support

Practical note: APN tier and Competency are necessary but not sufficient signals. A Premier partner with 3,000 certified staff globally may staff your project with junior consultants who have little hands-on experience. Always ask for specific team member CVs, relevant certifications, and worked examples from similar projects before engaging.
APN from the Customer Perspective

As an AWS customer, you interact with the APN whenever you hire a consulting firm for an AWS project, purchase software through AWS Marketplace (most Marketplace vendors are APN Technology Partners), or receive an AWS recommendation for a third-party product or service.

AWS increasingly enables the APN as an extension of its own sales and delivery motion. The AWS Customer Engagement (ACE) program allows partners to co-sell alongside AWS account teams — when a partner identifies an opportunity, they can register it in ACE, and the AWS account team may actively help close the deal. From the customer's perspective, this means you may get introduced to relevant partner expertise through your AWS account team, or vice versa.
Partner-Led vs AWS Direct Engagements
When you need implementation help for AWS, you have three options:
AWS Professional Services (ProServe): AWS's own consulting arm. Deep AWS expertise, direct access to product teams. Expensive; focused on strategic and complex engagements; limited capacity.
Premier or Advanced APN Consulting Partner: similar expertise for many services; more capacity; often more competitive pricing for execution-heavy work; may specialize in your industry.
Select or Registered APN Partner: smaller firms; variable quality; may excel in specific niches; often better for constrained-scope projects.

For most organizations, the practical choice is between AWS ProServe for initial architecture and governance work, and APN Consulting Partners for ongoing implementation, migration execution, and managed operations. The two are complementary and often co-deliver on large programs.
The APN and Your Career

If you work for an AWS partner organization, your personal AWS certifications directly affect the company's APN tier. Each AWS certification an employee earns contributes to the certification count that determines whether the company qualifies for Select, Advanced, or Premier status. This creates a direct financial incentive for partner companies to fund employee certification: a company one Associate certification short of the Advanced tier threshold may sponsor a training bootcamp for the entire team.

APN partner status also affects the resources available for your own professional development. Advanced and Premier partners have better access to AWS technical account managers, Solutions Architects, and specialized training programs. Working at a Premier partner gives you more opportunities to work on complex, cutting-edge AWS projects and to engage directly with AWS product teams — both of which accelerate skill development faster than working at a lower-tier partner or as an independent.
The AWS Competency Validation Process

AWS Competencies are not self-reported. A partner displaying a Competency badge has passed a formal validation process that typically takes 3-6 months and requires evidence across multiple dimensions. Understanding the rigor of this process helps you calibrate how much weight to give Competency status when selecting a partner.
Technical Validation
The technical validation component of a Competency application requires the partner to submit:
Detailed solution architecture documentation demonstrating how their solution or practice uses AWS services correctly and at depth
AWS Well-Architected Framework review reports showing that their solution designs pass the five pillars (operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, cost optimization)
AWS Foundational Technical Review (FTR) results for Technology Partners — a structured security and architecture review conducted by AWS
Live demos or recorded walkthroughs of the solution with AWS technical staff present
Evidence of AWS-specific security practices: IAM least privilege, encryption in transit and at rest, VPC isolation, GuardDuty and Security Hub enablement
Customer Reference Requirements

Most Competency applications require multiple customer references — typically 3-5 customers who have implemented the partner's solution or engaged the partner's services. AWS validates references through interviews or written questionnaires covering:
What specific AWS services were used in the engagement
What business outcomes were achieved (cost reduction, performance improvement, migration success)
Whether the partner demonstrated deep AWS expertise beyond surface-level familiarity
Whether the partner followed AWS best practices for security, reliability, and architecture
Customer satisfaction ratings

This reference validation requirement is why Competency badges are meaningful signals. A partner cannot purchase or shortcut this validation — they must have actual, satisfied customers who will speak to their specific AWS expertise.
APN Customer Engagements (ACE) Program

The APN Customer Engagements (ACE) program is the co-selling mechanism that connects APN partners with AWS sales teams. When you work with an APN partner who has registered your project in ACE, an AWS account manager or sales specialist may become directly involved in helping your project succeed — providing AWS credits, fast-tracking support requests, or connecting you to AWS subject matter experts.
How ACE Works from the Customer Side

From your perspective as a customer, ACE usually manifests in one of two ways: your AWS account team introduces you to a partner, or a partner you are already working with mentions that they have co-sell support from AWS. In both cases, the AWS team can:
Provide architecture reviews from AWS Solutions Architects on the project scope
Fast-track requests for AWS Partner Credits to fund proof-of-concept work
Escalate support cases related to the project to higher-tier support queues
Connect the partner with AWS service team members for advanced technical guidance
Provide AWS training resources for the partner team on the specific project

Not every project gets ACE involvement — it depends on the project size, the partner tier, and the specific relationship between the partner and the AWS account team. Large enterprise migrations or significant new AWS deployments are the most common cases where ACE co-sell is activated.
Well-Architected Partner Program

The AWS Well-Architected Framework (WAF) provides best practices across five pillars for evaluating and designing cloud architectures. The Well-Architected Partner Program gives APN Consulting Partners the ability to perform formal Well-Architected Reviews on behalf of their customers.

A WAF review is a structured assessment of an existing AWS workload against the five pillars. The output is a report identifying risks (high, medium, low) and specific improvement recommendations. AWS provides a tool (the AWS Well-Architected Tool) in the console that guides the review and generates the report.

Partners who are members of the Well-Architected Partner Program can conduct these reviews as a billable service, and in some cases AWS provides funding to their customers to offset the cost. For customers, this means:
You can ask any APN Consulting Partner about their Well-Architected review capability before engaging
A WAF review from a Competency-validated partner is more credible than one from an unvalidated partner, since the Competency evaluation itself includes WAF rigor checks
AWS sometimes offers funded Well-Architected Reviews to customers who are migrating to AWS or expanding their footprint
The Well-Architected Tool in the AWS console lets you run a self-guided review without a partner at all — useful for smaller workloads or internal teams building WAF expertise
APN in Enterprise Procurement
For enterprise IT organizations with formal procurement processes, APN status affects vendor evaluation and contract negotiation in concrete ways:
RFP Evaluation Criteria
When issuing a Request for Proposal for an AWS consulting engagement, including APN tier and Competency requirements creates a meaningful baseline:
Requiring Advanced or Premier tier filters out small firms without demonstrated AWS commitment
Requiring a specific Competency (e.g., Security Competency for a security architecture engagement) ensures domain validation
Requiring a minimum number of AWS-certified staff at the Professional and Specialty level ensures practical knowledge over partner status alone
Marketplace Private Offers in Procurement

For Technology Partners, purchasing via AWS Marketplace Private Offers simplifies procurement significantly. Instead of a separate software procurement process with legal, finance, and procurement teams, the Marketplace Private Offer flows through the AWS billing relationship. Many enterprise IT organizations have pre-approved vendor categories for AWS Marketplace purchases, which can reduce the time from evaluation to deployment from weeks to days.
Becoming an APN Partner

If you work for a company considering joining the APN — a software startup, a consultancy, or an internal shared services group that wants to formalize its AWS practice — the process starts at aws.amazon.com/partners/become-a-partner. The basic steps:
Create an account in the AWS Partner Network portal at partner.amazon.com
Complete the partner agreement and company profile
Register as the appropriate partner type (Technology or Consulting) and indicate target specializations
Begin accumulating the certifications, solutions, and revenue that move you toward Select tier
Apply for Competencies or Service Delivery designations as the practice matures

The time from Registered to Select typically takes 3-6 months for a focused team. The time to Advanced depends heavily on the pace of AWS revenue growth and certification investment. Premier tier often requires 2+ years of consistent investment in certifications, solution validation, and customer engagements.
AWS Training for Partners
APN partners receive discounts on AWS official training, and higher-tier partners receive free training credits. AWS offers several training programs specifically for partners:
AWS Partner Learning Path: role-based learning paths (solutions architect, developer, DevOps) tailored to partner delivery contexts
AWS Partner Transformation Program: business and practice development training for partner leadership
AWS Competency-specific training: domain-focused technical content required for Competency applications
AWS re:Skill and re:Train: training programs embedded in AWS events designed for partner technical staff
Partner Success Stories and Case Studies

AWS publishes hundreds of APN partner success stories on aws.amazon.com, organized by industry, technology domain, and partner. Reading these case studies before engaging a partner for a similar project gives you realistic expectations for outcomes, timelines, and approaches.

Case studies published on AWS.com have been co-reviewed by AWS and the partner. They typically include the business challenge, the architecture used, the specific AWS services deployed, and quantified business outcomes — cost reduction percentage, performance improvement, time-to-market reduction. They are a more reliable signal than testimonials on the partner's own website because the customer reference was verified through the APN review process.

When evaluating potential partners, ask them for AWS-published case studies in your industry and at your scale. If a partner claims Security Competency but cannot point to an AWS-published case study of a security implementation that resembles your environment, that is worth probing in reference checks.
APN vs Other Cloud Partner Programs
Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud both operate equivalent partner programs. Understanding how APN compares helps if your organization has multi-cloud requirements or is evaluating cloud providers:
Dimension
AWS APN
Microsoft CSP / MPN
Google Cloud Partner Advantage
Partner count
130,000+ globally
400,000+ globally (broader legacy IT footprint)
60,000+ globally
Tier structure
Registered to Premier (4 levels)
Registered to Gold; Solutions Partner for cloud
Member to Premier (3 levels)
Specialization badges
Competencies, Service Delivery, MSP
Azure Expert MSP, specializations
Expertise designations
Co-sell program
ACE (APN Customer Engagements)
Microsoft Marketplace co-sell
Google Cloud co-sell
Partner directory
partner.amazonaws.com (Partner Finder)
Microsoft AppSource / Partner Directory
Google Cloud Partner Directory
Marketplace
AWS Marketplace; Private Offers
Azure Marketplace; AppSource
Google Cloud Marketplace

The APN's relative maturity compared to Google Cloud's partner program reflects AWS's eight-year head start in cloud. For complex AWS workloads, the APN has more Premier-tier partners with validated deep expertise simply because there has been more time for partners to accumulate certifications, revenue, and customer references. For organizations standardizing on Azure or GCP, the respective partner programs are the relevant reference points.

One dimension where Microsoft's partner program historically had an advantage: reseller economics. The Microsoft Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) program allowed partners to resell Azure services with a billing margin, which is a more developed reseller channel model than AWS's equivalent. AWS has been building out its reseller channel capabilities in the APN over time, but this is an area where partner financial incentives differ significantly across cloud providers.
Common Questions When Working with APN Partners
Practitioners frequently encounter questions about APN partners in the context of selecting vendors or managing engagements. These come up repeatedly in enterprise cloud programs:
Does APN tier guarantee delivery quality?

No. Tier validates commitment, investment, and baseline capability — not delivery quality on your specific project. A Premier partner with 5,000 AWS-certified employees globally may staff your project with junior staff if the engagement is not large enough to attract senior resources. Always ask specifically: who will be on-site or in the team, what are their specific certifications and relevant experience, and can you meet key team members before signing.
Can a Registered partner deliver quality work?

Yes. Many specialized boutique firms with deep expertise in a niche domain are Registered or Select tier simply because they do not prioritize building a large certified headcount or accumulating AWS revenue in the categories AWS tracks. A three-person consulting firm that has been building Kubernetes infrastructure on AWS since 2015 and has 30 successful projects may outperform a Premier partner's team on an EKS engagement. Tier is a baseline filter, not a ceiling.
What happens when a partner's certification count drops?

APN requirements are evaluated annually. If a Consulting Partner's certified employee count falls below the minimum for their current tier — due to employee turnover or certifications expiring without renewal — AWS can downgrade the partner's tier. In practice, partners actively track this metric and fund certification renewals. When evaluating a partner, ask when their next APN renewal is and how confident they are in maintaining their certification count.

Rule of thumb for partner selection: Start with Competency and Service Delivery designations (validated expertise), then filter by tier (commitment and scale), then evaluate team composition (who actually does the work). The third step matters most for project outcomes.
Summary

The AWS Partner Network is a structured ecosystem of 130,000+ companies that extend AWS's reach into domains where customers need expertise beyond what AWS sells directly. Two tracks — Technology Partners (ISVs building products) and Consulting Partners (firms delivering services) — each progress through Registered, Select, Advanced, and Premier tiers based on certifications, revenue, validated solutions, and customer outcomes.

For customers, the APN provides a structured way to find qualified help: Competency and Service Delivery designations validate domain-specific expertise; tier indicates commitment and scale; AWS Partner Finder surfaces the directory. For practitioners, understanding APN mechanics helps you evaluate consulting firms intelligently and, if you work for a partner, shows how your certifications directly contribute to the company's tier and the benefits that come with it.
